items.title: Scaling and normalization (canonical scaler definitions)
items.description: StandardScaler and MinMaxScaler formulas, sklearn APIs, and train-only fit rules are canonical in scaling and normalization. This topic links there for math and owns pipeline placement after feature engineering.

items.title: Cleaning and transformation orchestration
items.description: Pipeline and ColumnTransformer step ordering—clean → impute → encode → scale → model—is orchestrated in cleaning and transformation. This topic specifies when the scale branch is required inside that layout for engineered features.

items.title: k-nearest neighbors
items.description: k-NN ranks neighbors by distance on whatever numeric columns the FE pipeline emits; without a scaler branch, the largest engineered column dominates similarity. Wire StandardScaler on numerics before fitting or querying neighbors.

items.title: Ridge and Lasso regularization
items.description: Penalized regression treats coefficients equally only when engineered features share comparable variance. Place StandardScaler on the continuous branch before Ridge or Lasso so λ reflects predictive contribution, not units.

items.title: Norms and Euclidean distance
items.description: L2 distance sums squared coordinate differences across engineered dimensions; rescaling is the FE pipeline step that prevents one column's units from defining the geometry.
